artifacts.title: Suspected neurological conditions: tremor in adults
assertions: Parkinsonian tremor suspicion is informed by unilateral or asymmetric rest tremor with stiffness, slowness, balance or gait change; suspected Parkinson's disease warrants specialist referral.
Suspected essential tremor assessment includes regular-medicine review, thyroid function and alcohol-use assessment; troublesome head tremor may merit movement-disorder referral.

artifacts.title: Parkinson's disease in adults
assertions: Tremor, stiffness, slowness, balance problems or gait disorders may prompt suspicion, but diagnosis is clinical and specialist-led.
This interview records supporting symptoms, function and medicine effects and does not diagnose or recommend medication changes.

artifacts.publisher: International Parkinson and Movement Disorder Society
artifacts.title: Consensus Statement on the Classification of Tremors
assertions: Axis 1 description uses historical and examination features including body distribution, activation condition, frequency and associated signs; Axis 2 concerns etiology.
The package captures patient-reportable Axis 1 features while preserving diagnostic and etiologic uncertainty.

artifacts.title: Tremor
assertions: Tremor may involve hands, arms, head, voice, torso or legs and may appear at rest or with posture and voluntary movement.
History includes onset, body distribution, provoking situations, medicines, substances, family history and functional effects.

artifacts.title: STOM tremor and movement terminology lookup
assertions: FHIR lookup confirmed active Tremor, Parkinsonism, Dystonia, Chorea and Myoclonus concepts in the 20260701 edition.
Terminology lookup and MRCM constrain representation only; they do not classify the observed movement or establish a diagnosis.
